findings:
## Item Hierarchy — Card Dashboard + Spider Web Visualizer

**EPIC**: Card-based Dashboard SPA with Spider Web Visualizer
- Priority: urgent-important | Tags: dashboard, spa, d3, tracing, extension, visualizer
- Single WebviewPanel, SPA via postMessage + webview.setState(). Fully additive.

**S1 — Card Dashboard Landing Page (SPA Refactor)**
- Priority: urgent-important | Paths: extension/src/webview/, media/dashboard.css
- T1.1: SPA router — postMessage routing + webview.setState() (webview/router.ts)
- T1.2: Card grid layout — CSS grid, card template, VS Code theme vars (dashboard.css, cards/card.ts)
- T1.3: Detail tab shell — back nav, content slot, breadcrumb, CSS transition (detailTab.ts)
- T1.4: Wire extension host postMessage handler for SPA navigation (extension.ts)

**S2 — TypeScript TraceLogger + FSWatcher Pipeline**
- Priority: urgent-important | Paths: shared/src/trace-event.ts, traceLogger.ts, extension/src/traceWatcher.ts
- T2.1: SpiderTraceEvent Zod schema in packages/shared (trace-event.ts)
- T2.2: TraceLogger JSONL writer — appendTraceEvent, atomic fs.appendFile (traceLogger.ts)
- T2.3: Export trace types from packages/shared index barrel
- T2.4: FSWatcher in extension host — tail last 10 lines, debounce 300ms, postMessage traceUpdate
- T2.5: Unit tests — Vitest for TraceLogger + FSWatcher pipeline

**S3 — Spider Web Visualizer Card (D3.js Radar Chart)**
- Priority: urgent-important | Depends on: S2 done + spike result
- Paths: extension/src/webview/cards/spiderWebCard.ts, src/agentRegistry.ts, esbuild.js, scripts/sync-agent-registry.mjs
- T3.1: Second esbuild entry — dashboard.ts → dist/webview/dashboard.js (iife, browser, d3 sub-packages ~120KB)
- T3.2: Agent registry TS constant — 31 agents, 35 edges + drift-prevention CI script
- T3.3: Radar chart mini-preview — 48x48 SVG, no D3 dep, ring/spoke dots, updates on traceUpdate
- T3.4: Detail tab — D3 animated radar chart 400x400, last 10 dispatches list, ring/domain labels
- T3.5: Wire traceUpdate into Spider Web card reactivity — mini-preview + detail re-render, loading state

**S4 — Mini-Preview Cards for Existing Sections + Documentation**
- Priority: not-urgent-important | Depends on: S1 card shell done
- T4.1: Burndown card — mini-preview progress bar + count badge
- T4.2: Eisenhower card — mini-preview 2x2 quadrant dot grid
- T4.3: Timeline card — mini-preview active items + next deadline
- T4.4: Traceability card — mini-preview link count + last-linked title
- T4.5: Stats card — mini-preview done% + in-progress count
- T4.6: Update extension/README.md — SPA dashboard + tracing docs
- T4.7: Update docs/architecture/mpd-architecture.md — tracing concepts + pipeline diagram

**SPIKE**: Investigate VS Code AI Toolkit / Copilot Chat API for Passive Agent Tracing
- Priority: urgent-important | Timebox: 1 day
- If viable: doc integration path, revise S3 T3.5. If not: confirm JSONL-write approach.

**LINKS**: S1/S2/S3/S4/spike implements EPIC; spike blocks S3; S2 blocks S3; S1 blocks S4

**IMPLEMENTATION ORDER**: spike+S1+S2 in parallel → S3 (after S2+spike) → S4 (after S1)

**TOTAL TASKS**: 21
recommendations: 1. PARALLEL: Dispatch spike + S1 + S2 simultaneously — all three are independent at kickoff.
2. GATE S3 on S2: Do not start Spider Web card until T2.4 FSWatcher passes tests and T3.1 esbuild entry is verified.
3. SPIKE MERGE: After spike closes, review findings before dispatching S3 T3.5; revise if passive API found.
4. GATE S4 on S1: All mini-preview card tasks depend on card shell (T1.2+T1.3); wait for S1 gate-reviewer PASS.
5. ADD sync-agent-registry.mjs to CI before merging S3 to prevent 31-agent/35-edge registry drift.
